description: The Five Families are the five major Italian American Mafia crime families operating in New York City, organized in 1931 by Salvatore Maranzano after the Castellammarese War. They are the Bonanno, Colombo, Gambino, Genovese, and Lucchese families, each with a hierarchy and territory, overseen by the Commission. Their existence was publicly disclosed by Joseph Valachi in 1963.
